- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so m…
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep…
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/…
- claude-api: Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, ge…
- mw-cp: Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process — pre-commit checks, doc updates, detailed commit, and push
- claude-mem:do: Execute a plan using subagents for implementation
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create an implementation plan with documentation discovery
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.

content.content:
=== PLAN ===
# MLPL Autograd v1 Saga (v0.5)

## Goal

Make differentiation a language primitive. Land a reverse-mode
autograd engine on the existing `DenseArray` substrate, expose
`tensor[...]`, `param[...]`, and `grad` at the language level,
and port the v0.4 demos that currently hand-write their
gradients onto the new engine. No new backends, no optimizers
beyond SGD, no model DSL -- those are later sagas.

## Quality requirements (every step)

1. TDD: failing tests first, then implementation, then refactor.
2. Quality gates must all pass before commit:
   - `cargo test` (all tests)
   - `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
   - `cargo fmt --all` + `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
   - `markdown-checker -f "**/*.md"` if docs touched
   - `sw-checklist`
3. Use `/mw-cp` checkpoint process.
4. Push immediately after commit.
5. Web UI changes rebuild `pages/` via `scripts/build-pages.sh`.

## What already exists

- `mlpl-array` DenseArray, shape, reductions, matmul
- `mlpl-runtime` built-ins (softmax, argmax, blobs, random, ...)
- `mlpl-eval` tree-walking evaluator
- `mlpl-parser` with current surface syntax
- v0.4 demos: `tiny_mlp.mlpl`, `softmax_classifier.mlpl`, ...
  (both hand-rolling gradient formulas)

## Phases

### Phase 1: Autograd core (Rust)
- New crate `mlpl-autograd` with a reverse-mode tape.
- `Tensor` wrapper holding `{ value: DenseArray, grad: RefCell<Option<DenseArray>>, node: NodeId }`.
- Graph nodes for: add, sub, mul, div, neg, matmul, sum,
  mean, exp, log, relu, tanh, sigmoid, softmax, transpose,
  broadcast, reshape.
- `backward(root)` walks the tape in reverse topo order and
  accumulates gradients into leaf tensors marked `requires_grad`.
- Tests: gradcheck against finite differences for every op.

### Phase 2: Language surface
- Parser support for `param[shape]` and `tensor[shape]`
  constructors (trainable vs. non-trainable leaves).
- New built-in `grad(expr, wrt)` where `wrt` is a parameter or
  list of parameters; returns array(s) matching their shape.
- Evaluator wires array-valued operations through the autograd
  tape when any input is a tracked tensor.
- Tests: end-to-end `grad` calls on scalar and vector losses.

### Phase 3: Demo ports
- Rewrite `demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl` to use `param` + `grad` instead
  of hand-written backprop. Must still pass the existing
  "MLP beats linear on XOR" integration test.
- Rewrite `demos/softmax_classifier.mlpl` the same way. Must
  still reach > 95% accuracy on separable blobs.
- Keep SGD manual for now (`W <- W - lr * grad`).

### Phase 4: Tutorial, docs, release
- Add tutorial lesson "Automatic differentiation" walking from
  a scalar example to the MLP port.
- Update `docs/milestone-ml.md` (or new `milestone-autograd.md`),
  `docs/saga.md`, `docs/status.md`, `docs/plan.md`.
- Bump REPL banner to v0.5.
- Rebuild and deploy `pages/`.
- Tag `v0.5.0-autograd`.

## Success criteria

- `mlpl-autograd` crate exists with gradcheck-verified ops.
- `grad` built-in works in the REPL for scalar and vector losses.
- Tiny MLP and softmax classifier demos pass their existing
  accuracy tests with zero hand-written gradient math.
- Tutorial lesson on autograd renders in the web REPL.
- All quality gates green, pages deployed, release tagged.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-autograd-crate-scaffold [production]: Create the mlpl-autograd crate skeleton. TDD. Add crate to workspace. Define ...
  [x] 002-autograd-elementwise-ops [production]: Add reverse-mode autograd for elementwise ops: add, sub, mul, div, neg, exp, ...
  [x] 003-autograd-reductions-matmul [production]: Add autograd for sum, mean, softmax, transpose, reshape, and matmul. TDD + gr...
  [x] 004-autograd-parser-surface [production]: Add parser support for param[shape] and tensor[shape] constructors. RED: pars...
  [x] 005-autograd-grad-builtin [production]: Add the grad(expr, wrt) built-in. wrt may be a single param or a list of para...
  [ ] 006-autograd-port-tiny-mlp [production]: Rewrite demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl to use param + grad instead of hand-written backp... <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 007-autograd-port-softmax-classifier [production]: Rewrite demos/softmax_classifier.mlpl to use param + grad. Must still reach >...
  [ ] 008-autograd-tutorial-lesson [production]: Add a tutorial lesson 'Automatic differentiation' to the web REPL tutorial sy...
  [ ] 009-autograd-release-v05 [production]: Release v0.5.0-autograd. Update docs/saga.md (Saga 9 COMPLETE), docs/status.m...

=== CURRENT STEP: 006-autograd-port-tiny-mlp [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Rewrite demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl to use param + grad instead of hand-written backp...
Task type: production

=== PROMPT ===
Rewrite demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl to use param + grad instead of hand-written backprop. Keep manual SGD updates (W <- W - lr * grad). The existing tiny_mlp integration test (MLP beats linear on XOR-style data) must still pass unchanged. Do not change the test. Commit via /mw-cp.

=== PAST SUCCESSES (production) ===
  [2026-04-07T18:18:40] action=RED tests first, built grad.rs with mini-evaluator lifting binops/unary ops/reductions/matmul onto autograd tape, split unary dispatch via fn pointer table to meet sw-checklist LOC limit, /mw-cp checkpoint, push, result=success, reward=+1
  [2026-04-07T18:12:23] action=RED parser+eval tests, GREEN AST+parser+eval impl, fixed sw-checklist fn count by inlining ctor-kind helper, /mw-cp checkpoint, push, result=success, reward=+1
  [2026-04-07T17:58:02] action=TDD gradcheck tests, closure-based softmax, module split into reduction_ops, inlined helpers, /mw-cp, result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.content: 1	# Tiny 1-hidden-layer MLP on an XOR-style non-linearly separable dataset.

file.content: # Tiny 1-hidden-layer MLP on an XOR-style non-linearly separable dataset.

input.content:
# Tiny 1-hidden-layer MLP on an XOR-style non-linearly separable dataset.
#
# This version uses MLPL's autograd: parameters are declared with
# `param[...]` and gradients are computed with the `grad` built-in.
# SGD updates stay manual (W <- W - lr * grad).
#
# A linear softmax classifier (also autograd-powered) is trained on
# the same data for comparison: the MLP learns a curved boundary,
# the linear model cannot.

# ----- data: 4 blobs arranged as XOR, labeled by diagonal -----
D   = blobs(3, 20, [[-2,-2],[2,2],[-2,2],[2,-2]])
X   = matmul(D, [[1,0],[0,1],[0,0]])
raw = reshape(matmul(D, [[0],[0],[1]]), [80])
y   = gt(raw, 1.5)
Y   = one_hot(y, 2)

# Precomputed broadcast helper: grad() can't call `ones` directly,
# so we lift it into an environment variable used as a const leaf
# inside the loss expression.
O80 = ones([80, 1])

# ----- linear softmax baseline (autograd + MSE-on-one-hot loss) -----
# Biases have shape [1, C] so they can be broadcast via matmul(O80, b).
Wl = param[2, 2]
bl = param[1, 2]
lr = 0.3
repeat 300 {
  Wl = Wl - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y)), Wl);
  bl = bl - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y)), bl)
}
logits      = matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)
Pl          = softmax(logits, 1)
linear_pred = argmax(Pl, 1)
linear_accuracy = mean(eq(linear_pred, y))
linear_accuracy
confusion_matrix(linear_pred, y)

# ----- tiny MLP: 2 -> 8 -> 2 with tanh + softmax -----
# Declare trainable parameters, then reseed with random values. The
# reassignment keeps the `param` mark so `grad` still tracks them.
W1 = param[2, 8]
b1 = param[1, 8]
W2 = param[8, 2]
b2 = param[1, 2]
W1 = randn(5, [2, 8]) * 0.5
W2 = randn(6, [8, 2]) * 0.5
lr = 0.2

# Training loop with loss-curve capture: 20 outer steps of 30 updates
# each. `counter` indexes into a length-20 losses vector via an
# iota/eq mask (MLPL has no array indexing, so we broadcast instead).
# Each grad() call re-evaluates the MSE loss on the current parameters.
counter = 0
losses  = zeros([20])
repeat 20 {
  repeat 30 {
    W1 = W1 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), W1);
    b1 = b1 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), b1);
    W2 = W2 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), W2);
    b2 = b2 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), b2)
  };
  Z1 = matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1);
  H  = tanh_fn(Z1);
  Z2 = matmul(H, W2) + matmul(O80, b2);
  P  = softmax(Z2, 1);
  loss = 0 - mean(reduce_add(Y * log(P + 0.0001), 1));
  mask = eq(iota(20), counter);
  losses = losses + loss * mask;
  counter = counter + 1
}

# ----- evaluation -----
Z1 = matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)
H  = tanh_fn(Z1)
Z2 = matmul(H, W2) + matmul(O80, b2)
P  = softmax(Z2, 1)
mlp_pred = argmax(P, 1)
mlp_accuracy = mean(eq(mlp_pred, y))
mlp_accuracy

loss_curve(losses)
confusion_matrix(mlp_pred, y)

# ----- MLP decision surface on a 30x30 grid -----
G   = grid([-4, 4, -4, 4], 30)
O900 = ones([900, 1])
GZ1 = matmul(G, W1) + matmul(O900, b1)
GH  = tanh_fn(GZ1)
GZ2 = matmul(GH, W2) + matmul(O900, b2)
GP  = softmax(GZ2, 1)
# Class-1 probability as the surface.
p1  = reshape(matmul(GP, [[0],[1]]), [900])
boundary_2d(p1, [30, 30], X, y)

toolUseResult.content:
# Tiny 1-hidden-layer MLP on an XOR-style non-linearly separable dataset.
#
# This version uses MLPL's autograd: parameters are declared with
# `param[...]` and gradients are computed with the `grad` built-in.
# SGD updates stay manual (W <- W - lr * grad).
#
# A linear softmax classifier (also autograd-powered) is trained on
# the same data for comparison: the MLP learns a curved boundary,
# the linear model cannot.

# ----- data: 4 blobs arranged as XOR, labeled by diagonal -----
D   = blobs(3, 20, [[-2,-2],[2,2],[-2,2],[2,-2]])
X   = matmul(D, [[1,0],[0,1],[0,0]])
raw = reshape(matmul(D, [[0],[0],[1]]), [80])
y   = gt(raw, 1.5)
Y   = one_hot(y, 2)

# Precomputed broadcast helper: grad() can't call `ones` directly,
# so we lift it into an environment variable used as a const leaf
# inside the loss expression.
O80 = ones([80, 1])

# ----- linear softmax baseline (autograd + MSE-on-one-hot loss) -----
# Biases have shape [1, C] so they can be broadcast via matmul(O80, b).
Wl = param[2, 2]
bl = param[1, 2]
lr = 0.3
repeat 300 {
  Wl = Wl - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y)), Wl);
  bl = bl - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y)), bl)
}
logits      = matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)
Pl          = softmax(logits, 1)
linear_pred = argmax(Pl, 1)
linear_accuracy = mean(eq(linear_pred, y))
linear_accuracy
confusion_matrix(linear_pred, y)

# ----- tiny MLP: 2 -> 8 -> 2 with tanh + softmax -----
# Declare trainable parameters, then reseed with random values. The
# reassignment keeps the `param` mark so `grad` still tracks them.
W1 = param[2, 8]
b1 = param[1, 8]
W2 = param[8, 2]
b2 = param[1, 2]
W1 = randn(5, [2, 8]) * 0.5
W2 = randn(6, [8, 2]) * 0.5
lr = 0.2

# Training loop with loss-curve capture: 20 outer steps of 30 updates
# each. `counter` indexes into a length-20 losses vector via an
# iota/eq mask (MLPL has no array indexing, so we broadcast instead).
# Each grad() call re-evaluates the MSE loss on the current parameters.
counter = 0
losses  = zeros([20])
repeat 20 {
  repeat 30 {
    W1 = W1 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), W1);
    b1 = b1 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), b1);
    W2 = W2 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), W2);
    b2 = b2 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), b2)
  };
  Z1 = matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1);
  H  = tanh_fn(Z1);
  Z2 = matmul(H, W2) + matmul(O80, b2);
  P  = softmax(Z2, 1);
  loss = 0 - mean(reduce_add(Y * log(P + 0.0001), 1));
  mask = eq(iota(20), counter);
  losses = losses + loss * mask;
  counter = counter + 1
}

# ----- evaluation -----
Z1 = matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)
H  = tanh_fn(Z1)
Z2 = matmul(H, W2) + matmul(O80, b2)
P  = softmax(Z2, 1)
mlp_pred = argmax(P, 1)
mlp_accuracy = mean(eq(mlp_pred, y))
mlp_accuracy

loss_curve(losses)
confusion_matrix(mlp_pred, y)

# ----- MLP decision surface on a 30x30 grid -----
G   = grid([-4, 4, -4, 4], 30)
O900 = ones([900, 1])
GZ1 = matmul(G, W1) + matmul(O900, b1)
GH  = tanh_fn(GZ1)
GZ2 = matmul(GH, W2) + matmul(O900, b2)
GP  = softmax(GZ2, 1)
# Class-1 probability as the surface.
p1  = reshape(matmul(GP, [[0],[1]]), [900])
boundary_2d(p1, [30, 30], X, y)

lines: # Tiny 1-hidden-layer MLP on an XOR-style non-linearly separable dataset.
-# Also trains a linear softmax classifier on the same data for
-# comparison: the MLP learns a curved boundary, the linear model can't.
+# This version uses MLPL's autograd: parameters are declared with
+# `param[...]` and gradients are computed with the `grad` built-in.
+# SGD updates stay manual (W <- W - lr * grad).
+# A linear softmax classifier (also autograd-powered) is trained on
+# the same data for comparison: the MLP learns a curved boundary,
# ----- data: 4 blobs arranged as XOR, labeled by diagonal -----
+# Precomputed broadcast helper: grad() can't call `ones` directly,
+# so we lift it into an environment variable used as a const leaf
+# Biases have shape [1, C] so they can be broadcast via matmul(O80, b).
+  Wl = Wl - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y)), Wl);
+  bl = bl - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(X, Wl) + matmul(O80, bl)) - Y)), bl)
+# Declare trainable parameters, then reseed with random values. The
+# reassignment keeps the `param` mark so `grad` still tracks them.
# Training loop with loss-curve capture: 20 outer steps of 30 updates
# each. `counter` indexes into a length-20 losses vector via an
# iota/eq mask (MLPL has no array indexing, so we broadcast instead).
+# Each grad() call re-evaluates the MSE loss on the current parameters.

+    W1 = W1 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), W1);

+    b1 = b1 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), b1);

+    W2 = W2 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), W2);

+    b2 = b2 - lr * grad(mean((softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y) * (softmax(matmul(tanh(matmul(X, W1) + matmul(O80, b1)), W2) + matmul(O80, b2)) - Y)), b2)
# ----- MLP decision surface on a 30x30 grid -----

content.content: Launching skill: mw-cp

content.text:
# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

---

## Usage
```
/mw-cp
/mw-cp adds OAuth token refresh with retry logic

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(autograd): port tiny_mlp demo to param + grad

Rewrite demos/tiny_mlp.mlpl to declare parameters with param[...]
and use the grad built-in instead of hand-written backprop. Both
the linear softmax baseline and the 2->8->2 tanh MLP now rely on
reverse-mode autograd; SGD updates stay manual (W <- W - lr * grad).

Biases use shape [1, C] so they can be broadcast via matmul(O80, b)
inside the grad expression (grad's mini-evaluator doesn't support
reshape or ones). The MSE-on-one-hot loss composes only ops that
the grad tape-lifter supports: matmul, tanh, softmax (unary, last
axis), elementwise arithmetic, and mean.

The existing tiny_mlp integration test is inline and untouched;
it continues to pass. Verified the demo runs end-to-end via the
REPL and emits the same loss-curve / decision-boundary outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push
